content: Faida: Gods, this heat’s got the whole tavern sweating like a whore in a temple. How ‘bout you, vampire? You ever get hot or is that undead cunt always ice cold? Serana: Fuck off, I burn hotter than a Skooma den at midnight when MJR’s hands are on me. Though speaking of heat—saw your boy Gaius eyeing Julienne’s tits earlier. That little Breton apprentice of yours might need a dagger lesson if he gets too handsy. Faida: That little shit better keep his fingers to himself unless he wants ‘em chopped off and fed to the horkers. You vampires aren’t the only ones who know how to drain a man dry. Serana: Please, darling, I’ve drained men drier than the Reach in a drought. But enough about blood—you hear about Jarl Elisif’s new “peace talks”? Bitch thinks we can fuck our way to harmony with the Stormcloaks. Faida: Ain’t no peace coming ‘til Ulfric’s throat’s slit and his corpse is gangbanged by angry Draugr. Empire might have stick-up-the-ass officers, but at least they pay their fucking bar tabs. Serana: Now that’s a political stance I can drink to—after MJR drinks from me first, of course. Speaking of, you ever let Gaius bend you over that bridge your village’s named after? The fall would be almost as good as the ride. Faida: Bridge is sturdier than my beds, sweetheart. Ask your bloodsucker sisters if they doubt it. Though I bet your Dragonborn’s had you screaming loud enough to shake loose a few stones yourself. Serana: Oh, he’s made this dead girl come alive more times than I can count—usually while Lydia watches and fists herself raw. But enough about my love life—when’s the last time you turned a profit off something besides watered-down mead and drunk lumberjack complaints? Faida: Hah! Profit’s overrated when you’ve got Horgeir sobbing into his ale about his wife taking Lodvar’s cock louder than a mammoth in heat.

Faida: Well shit, this tavern ain’t gonna run itself. Julienne better not be slacking off again, or I’ll tan her Breton hide. Serana: Hope your evening’s as entertaining as your insults, love. I’ve got a certain Dragonborn to track down—preferably before Lydia gets her grubby hands on him first. Faida: Good luck, fang-whore. Try not to start a blood riot on your way out. Serana: No promises.
Lydia: Ugh, this tavern stinks of weak Imperial dogs and cheap ale. At least the view of that bridge ain't bad.
